objective: Students will understand compression hash functions, including their construction (e.g., Merkle-Damgård), security properties (collision resistance, preimage resistance, second-preimage resistance), and practical applications in cryptographic protocols.

subtopics.title: Compression Functions – Definition, Role, and Hands-On Exploration
subtopics.description: Introduces compression functions as foundational building blocks in hash functions. Students will learn their role in reducing input size and ensuring deterministic outputs through structured experiments.
subtopics.teaching_approach: Structured practice (guided 4-bit compressor simulation), retrieval practice (flashcards), and metaphorical framing ('digital translators').

subtopics.title: Merkle-Damgård Construction – Structure, Mechanics, and Labyrinth Metaphor
subtopics.description: Explains the Merkle-Damgård framework with iterative processing, padding, and chaining, framed as a 'guardian’s labyrinth' for data security.
subtopics.teaching_approach: Worked examples (color-coded diagrams), analogical reasoning ('digital relay race'), and structured practice (padding rule modification).

subtopics.title: Collision Resistance – Definition, Real-World Implications, and Debate
subtopics.description: Defines collision resistance and explores real-world scenarios (e.g., forged digital certificates) through contrast-case analysis and debate.
subtopics.teaching_approach: Contrast-case analysis (SHA-1 vs. SHA-256), debate prompts ('Is SHA-1 safe for password storage?'), and retrieval practice (multiple-choice questions).

subtopics.title: Preimage and Second-Preimage Resistance – Distinctions, Applications, and Trade-Offs
subtopics.description: Compares preimage and second-preimage resistance, emphasizing trade-offs between security and computational efficiency.
subtopics.teaching_approach: Structured worksheets (scenario-based questions), metaphors ('locked vault' vs. 'fingerprint'), and trade-off analysis.

subtopics.title: Practical Applications – Blockchain, Digital Signatures, and Historical Context
subtopics.description: Connects hash function properties to real-world protocols (blockchain, digital signatures) with historical narratives (e.g., Ralph Merkle’s 1979 paper).
subtopics.teaching_approach: Case studies (blockchain data integrity), collaborative discussions ('How would a collision attack compromise a digital signature?'), and historical storytelling.

subtopics.title: Evaluating Hash Function Security – Structured Analysis and Real-World Tools
subtopics.description: Teaches learners to assess hash functions using security properties with hands-on tools (e.g., `hashlib`, online hash crackers).
subtopics.teaching_approach: Structured practice (tool-based collision testing), guided worksheets (analyzing MD5/SHA-1), and metaphor ('detective’s checklist').

subtopics.title: Synthesis – Designing Secure Hash Protocols with Peer Review
subtopics.description: Integrates knowledge of compression functions, construction, and security properties to design basic hash-based protocols with peer critique.
subtopics.teaching_approach: Problem-based learning (protocol design), peer review (rubric-based critique), and reflection ('What assumptions did you make?').
